abstract: <jats:p>This chapter offers an exploration of the skill and embodied knowledge in the historical processes of making (botanical) printing woodblocks through a documentary approach of the applied performative method of historical remaking. It recounts the processes of experimenting with historical woodcutting techniques to remake four sixteenth-century woodblocks that carry botanical images. It delves into the bases of the woods, tools, and techniques of cutting relief printing blocks in the early modern period through examinations of historical text, images, and artefacts. Experimenting and writing from the perspective of a novice maker, this chapter pinpoints the skills and embodied knowledge that are essential in the process of early modern woodcutting.</jats:p>
title: Remaking Sixteenth-Century Botanical Woodblocks: Embodied Artisanal Knowledge in Early Modern Woodcutting
container-title: Embodied Experiences of Making in Early Modern Europe
